title: Adapter Manifest
description: Schema for adapter.json manifest files used by the atrium Script Adapter SDK.

sdkVersion.description: SDK version this adapter targets. v2 is current (lean method set, binaryDiscovery + skillInstallPath on the manifest); v1 is accepted for legacy adapters.

displayName.description: Human-readable adapter name shown in the UI.

accent.description: Hex color code for the adapter accent color.

binary.description: Name of the CLI binary this adapter wraps (e.g., 'claude', 'codex').

methods.description: Map of method names to their resolution references.

script.description: Path to the executable script file, relative to the adapter directory.

static.description: Path to a static JSON file, relative to the adapter directory.

hooks.description: SDK v2. Optional map of hook event names (kebab-case, e.g. 'session-start') to stable atrium:// URIs. When present, atrium passes ATRIUM_HOOK_URI_* env vars to hooks.sh install so the installer can bake them into the tool's hook config.

hookEnvelopes.description: SDK v2. Optional. Declares the per-event response-envelope shape atrium core emits when the underlying tool fires SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, PreToolUse, and PostToolUse hooks. This is ALSO the per-event injection-capability matrix (Epic 77): for each event, 'kind: none' (or an omitted field) means the adapter CANNOT have context injected back into that event ('cannot inject unless verified' — the conservative default); any non-none 'kind' means the adapter IS injection-capable for that event and atrium renders the assembled context in that envelope shape. Lets atrium's context-injection layer stay adapter-agnostic — new adapters declare their capability + envelope here rather than requiring a Rust enum bump. Omitted fields fall back to atrium's default (identity for sessionStartManifest, hookSpecificOutput/UserPromptSubmit with systemMessage for userPromptSubmit, and PreToolUse/PostToolUse incapable — the Claude-Code-compatible defaults).

sessionStartManifest.description: Envelope wrapping the SessionStart manifest stdout. 'identity' = raw text/plain (Claude Code, Codex, Grok); 'hookSpecificOutput' = JSON {hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName, additionalContext}} (Gemini); 'flatAdditionalContext' = JSON {additional_context} (Cursor); 'none' = empty body (skip injection).

userPromptSubmit.description: Envelope wrapping the sigil-resolution response for the UserPromptSubmit (or equivalent) hook. 'hookSpecificOutput' is the Claude-Code-shaped envelope used by claude-code/codex/gemini/grok. 'none' is used by adapters with no same-turn injection primitive (cursor-agent).

includeSystemMessage.description: When true, the envelope also includes a top-level 'systemMessage' field with the resolution status line. Defaults to false. Set true for claude-code/gemini/grok; false for codex.

preToolUse.description: Epic 77 Story 77.5. Envelope + injection-capability for the PreToolUse hook (atrium's RunCommandStatusProvider injects a terse 'these run-commands are already running' nudge here, before a shell-class tool call). 'hookSpecificOutput' is the Claude-Code-shaped envelope (capable: atrium renders the assembled nudge in it). 'none' (or an omitted preToolUse field) means the adapter CANNOT inject at PreToolUse — the conservative default. Only set a non-none kind for an adapter VERIFIED to consume injected PreToolUse context.

includeSystemMessage.description: When true, the envelope also includes a top-level 'systemMessage' field. Defaults to false.

postToolUse.description: Epic 78 Story 78.1/78.3. Envelope + injection-capability for the PostToolUse hook (the carried hook payload is the tool RESULT rather than the tool input). 'hookSpecificOutput' is the Claude-Code-shaped envelope (capable: atrium renders the assembled injection in it). 'none' (or an omitted postToolUse field) means the adapter CANNOT inject at PostToolUse — the conservative default. Only set a non-none kind for an adapter VERIFIED to consume injected PostToolUse context. Per-adapter capability is declared in Story 78.3.

includeSystemMessage.description: When true, the envelope also includes a top-level 'systemMessage' field. Defaults to false.

binaryDiscovery.description: SDK v2. Replaces the v1 detect_binary.sh method. atrium walks 'commands' against PATH and falls back to 'wellKnownPaths' to locate the CLI binary.

commands.description: Candidate binary names to search on PATH, in priority order.

wellKnownPaths.description: Absolute or ~-prefixed fallback paths probed when no command resolves on PATH.

install.description: SDK v2. Optional. Per-platform install recipe atrium runs (in a visible managed terminal) from the Tools UI to install the adapter's CLI binary when it isn't found. Keys are platform ids; an absent platform key means atrium has no recipe for that OS and falls back to the manual 'Locate binary…' path. When the recipe field is omitted entirely the Install button is unavailable for every platform.

cmd.description: Shell command line atrium runs to install the binary (e.g. 'npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code').

postInstallAuth.description: When true, the tool requires an interactive sign-in after install; atrium surfaces the Binary-row 'Sign-in required' note.

cmd.description: Shell command line atrium runs to install the binary (e.g. 'npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code').

postInstallAuth.description: When true, the tool requires an interactive sign-in after install; atrium surfaces the Binary-row 'Sign-in required' note.

cmd.description: Shell command line atrium runs to install the binary (e.g. 'npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code').

postInstallAuth.description: When true, the tool requires an interactive sign-in after install; atrium surfaces the Binary-row 'Sign-in required' note.

skillInstallPath.description: SDK v2. Absolute or ~-prefixed path where atrium installs the adapter's SKILL.md (e.g. '~/.claude/skills/atrium/skill.md').

skillsDir.description: SDK v2. Absolute or ~-prefixed path to the underlying tool's skills directory (e.g. '~/.claude/skills'). Used by atrium for skill installation discovery.

icon.description: SDK v2. Optional filename (relative to the adapter directory) of an SVG icon to display in atrium's UI. The file's contents are inlined into the adapter DTO at load time. Conventions: (1) root <svg> element with a viewBox attribute (atrium strips width/height and sets them at render time); (2) shapes use fill="currentColor" (or stroke="currentColor") so the icon recolors against the theme; (3) no <script>, no on* attributes, no <foreignObject>, no external references — atrium sanitizes but well-formed input avoids surprises. When omitted, the adapter falls back to atrium's curated brand-icon map keyed off the adapter name, then to a colored dot.

retention.description: SDK v2. Optional. Declares the underlying tool's session retention knobs so atrium can render a 'click to extend' chip in the adapter pane footer and write new values via the tool's own config file. Adapters whose tool has no retention cap omit this field entirely; the chip is skipped.

fields.description: List of retention knobs. Each entry describes one numeric setting (days, max sessions, etc.) the user can extend.

key.description: Identifier passed back to the writeScript as key=value.

label.description: Human-readable label shown in the modal.

unit.description: Short suffix shown after the value in the chip (e.g. 'd' for days, 's' for sessions).

default.description: Documented default the underlying tool uses out of the box.

recommended.description: Threshold at or above which the retention chip is hidden. Also pre-filled in the modal.

readScript.description: Path (relative to the adapter dir) of a script that prints the current retention values as a JSON object keyed by field key. Missing keys are treated as the field's default.

writeScript.description: Path (relative to the adapter dir) of a script that accepts key=value pairs as positional args (e.g. 'days=365 maxCount=9999') and updates the tool's config file.

sessionExtractor.description: SDK v2. Optional. Declares an adapter-owned script that extracts native-transcript content into the canonical event stream consumed by atrium's session FTS index. When present, atrium's indexer invokes the script per session at backfill + live-watch time. See canonical-event.schema.json for the JSONL contract.

script.description: Path (relative to the adapter dir) of the extractor script. Convention: 'extract_session.sh'.

schemaVersion.description: Canonical-event schema version this script emits. Hashed into the FTS index row's extractor_version so version bumps trigger per-adapter reindex on idle.

supportsDepths.description: Depth presets this script supports. Adapters MAY declare a subset if their transcript format only supports a degraded extraction (e.g. ['quick'] for an unstable transcript format).
